stories.title: Sony’s decision to ditch discs was practically inevitable, data shows
stories.summary: Sony has announced it will permanently discontinue production of physical discs for all new PlayStation games by the end of 2027, with all newly released titles available exclusively in digital formats from January 2028. This decision marks the culmination of a 16-year decline in physical game sales, with the trend of downloadable options becoming increasingly prevalent since the late 2000s. The shift to digital is expected to continue, with major releases like Grand Theft Auto VI already offering "download in a box" retail options.

stories.title: Burnham urged to lobby EU leaders directly to waive EES border controls
stories.summary: British holidaymakers are expected to face long queues at European borders this weekend, with around 2.2 million people traveling between Friday and Monday. The Liberal Democrats are urging Prime Minister Andy Burnham to lobby EU leaders to suspend the new entry-exit system, citing its malfunctioning state, which has caused delays and missed flights. If resolved, this could alleviate the summer travel chaos for UK citizens.

stories.title: Joe Manchin reenters the political orbit with a new independent experiment
stories.summary: Joe Manchin, a former West Virginia senator, has launched the Independent Leadership Council to recruit and support independent candidates nationwide. The initiative aims to back individuals who can vote with either Democrats or Republicans when necessary and speak out against them when they are wrong. Manchin's effort comes as a record 45% of American adults identify as political independents, reflecting growing disillusionment with the major parties.

stories.title: India’s Paid Music Subscribers Could Hit 30 Million by 2028, EY-IMI Report Finds
stories.summary: India's paid music streaming subscriber base is expected to reach between 28 and 30 million by 2028, up from 14 million in December 2025, according to a recent report by EY and the Indian Music Industry. This growth is attributed to the country's large smartphone user base, with 96% of owners consuming music and 80% listening for over an hour daily. The report highlights a significant gap between music and video streaming, with 38% of users paying for music versus 86% for video streaming services.

stories.title: Burnham to spend first day working from northern branch of No 10
stories.summary: UK Deputy Prime Minister Andy Burnham has begun working from the northern branch of No 10, marking a significant shift in the country's power dynamics. Located in Manchester, the hub will serve as a central meeting point for government ministers from across the UK, with 10 ministers scheduled to meet there on Friday. This move aims to promote regional equality and reduce the need for frequent trips to London.

stories.title: Tens of thousands evacuated from French tourist spot and near Madrid as wildfires spread
stories.summary: Tens of thousands of people have been evacuated from a French tourist spot and near Madrid as wildfires spread, with over 23,000 people forced to leave their homes in the Landes region of France. The fires, fueled by strong winds of up to 31mph and temperatures of 36-37C, have destroyed at least 105 homes and buildings, with no reported casualties overnight. The European Union has dispatched aid to help combat the fires, which are expected to continue raging over the weekend.

stories.title: TikTok’s protection of minors should not be opt-in, warns EU
stories.summary: The European Union has warned TikTok that its default settings for minors' accounts are inadequate, allowing their content to be visible to other users and potentially exposing them to harm. The Commission suggests that TikTok should change its settings so that minors' content is only visible to users they have chosen to share it with. If TikTok fails to address these concerns, it could face fines of up to 6 percent of its global annual revenue.

stories.title: Why are UK fuel prices rising again?
stories.summary: UK petrol prices have reached their highest level since mid-June, with the average cost of fuel now exceeding previous records due to the ongoing US-Israel war with Iran. Wholesale oil prices have risen above $100 a barrel, directly impacting the cost of petrol and diesel at the pump, with every $10 increase pushing up prices by roughly 7p a litre. Unless the hostilities end soon, fuel prices are likely to continue rising, affecting drivers' finances.

stories.title: Kino Rebelde Boards Locarno Title ‘September Afternoon’ for International Sales (EXCLUSIVE)
stories.summary: Kino Rebelde has acquired international sales rights for "September Afternoon", a film by writer-director Nicolaas Schmidt, which premiered at the Locarno Film Festival's Cineasti del Presente competition section. The movie follows a couple's desperate attempt to cling to summer, only to face a disaster of a different magnitude. Kino Rebelde's María Vera will now oversee the film's international distribution.

stories.title: South Africa's president wins bid to temporarily halt impeachment inquiry over 'Farmgate'
stories.summary: South Africa's President Cyril Ramaphosa has won a temporary reprieve from an impeachment inquiry over the "Farmgate" scandal, with a court ruling in his favour to halt the parliamentary investigation. The inquiry stems from a 2022 incident where burglars stole large sums of cash hidden in a sofa from Ramaphosa's private farm, which he denies any wrongdoing in. The president will now challenge the findings of an independent panel in a separate court case to be heard in September.

stories.title: Edinburgh Industry Program Features Ewan McGregor, Kenneth Branagh and Bruce Dern and an Inaugural U.K. Film Conference
stories.summary: The Edinburgh International Film Festival's 2026 industry program has announced a star-studded lineup featuring Ewan McGregor, Kenneth Branagh, and Bruce Dern, among others, to participate in the inaugural UK Film Conference, a five-day event aimed at discussing the state of British independent cinema. The conference will take place in Edinburgh, with over 50 industry professionals and experts in attendance. The event is expected to spark crucial discussions on the future of British film production.

stories.title: Preston Bus still to decide on Burnham's £2 cap
stories.summary: Preston Bus is still to decide on joining a new government scheme capping single fares at £2 from January, as it awaits reimbursement details for participating operators. The scheme, backed by £400m, aims to help passengers with the cost of living, particularly in rural areas where single fares can be higher. The bus company will assess the plan once reimbursement arrangements are available, with no decision on participation announced yet.

stories.title: Devlin opens up on 'too stressful' Hearts exit for Rangers
stories.summary: Rangers midfielder Alex Devlin has opened up on his decision to leave Hearts, citing the stress of the World Cup and contract negotiations as factors in his departure. Devlin, who failed to add to his five Australia caps at the World Cup, has joined Rangers after Hearts announced he would not be signing a new contract. He will now link up with manager John McInnes, who had previously tried to persuade him to stay at Hearts.

stories.title: Klopp starts Germany reign by threatening to quit
stories.summary: German football coach Jurgen Klopp has begun his tenure by warning the media that he will quit if they invade his family's privacy. He made the threat during his first press conference as Germany's head coach, a position he holds on a four-year deal until the 2030 World Cup. Klopp's appointment was widely expected and he has stated that this is the pinnacle of his coaching career, with no plans to continue after leaving the national team.

stories.title: India's 'cockroach' protesters hold talks with government as stalemate continues
stories.summary: Thousands of protesters in India are continuing their demands for the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan, following talks with the government that failed to yield a decision on the key issue. The Cockroach Janta Party, a satirical movement turned powerful platform for young people's dissent, has seen its protests gain momentum after a police crackdown left dozens injured. The government has agreed to consider two other demands, including compensation for families of students who died by suicide and the withdrawal of police cases against protesters.

stories.title: Russia and Ukraine Trade Blows With Missiles and Drones
stories.summary: Ukrainian strikes set fire to warehouses and other sites in Russia, and Russian bombing rocked Ukraine’s capital, as the two countries escalated a deadly air campaign.

stories.title: I spent years trying to get smaller. Until I decided to get bigger
stories.summary: A woman's decision to get married triggered a body-loathing spiral, prompting her to start tracking her weight and calories, and restricting her food intake, with some days resulting in binge eating. She had previously avoided weighing herself for years but began daily weigh-ins after her proposal. Her wedding planning process led to a relapse of unhealthy eating habits she had not experienced since her teenage years.

stories.title: With Hopes High for New H.I.V. Prevention Pill, Merck Takes Steps to Ensure Access
stories.summary: Companies in Africa and India will make generic versions of the pill that could cost as little as $5 per person per year. But access to the drug and other new products in much of Latin America remains uncertain.

stories.title: Trump’s Global Tariffs: What’s the Latest on the Trade War
stories.summary: The president has unveiled a complicated system to forge ahead with his trade war, after the Supreme Court struck down his original levies.

stories.title: US and Iran trade more strikes in Gulf as fears of escalation mount
stories.summary: US and Iranian forces have engaged in a new round of airstrikes in the Middle East, with Iran reporting drone strikes on US military facilities and bases across the region, resulting in at least four deaths and five injuries. The escalation comes as Iran condemns the US plan to use frozen Iranian assets to pay for war damages, with Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi calling the move an incendiary precedent. The situation remains highly volatile, with reports of further strikes and disruptions to air travel in the region.

stories.title: One year, 17 towns, dozens of voices: what we heard along the coast of England and Wales
stories.summary: A devastating 73% decline in funding for youth services in England over the past 12 years has left nearly 1 million 16- to 24-year-olds without work, education, or training, with many young people in coastal towns feeling isolated and despondent due to a lack of opportunities. This stark reality was witnessed firsthand by a team reporting on the lives of young people in coastal communities across England and Wales. The situation has been exacerbated by decades of negative media portrayals of these young people and their towns.

stories.title: Rare night heron spotted at National Trust site
stories.summary: A rare black-crowned night heron has been spotted at the National Trust's Holnicote Estate in Somerset, a first for the UK, raising hopes that conservation efforts to restore freshwater habitats will provide a refuge for the bird and other wildlife. The estate's new habitats have also attracted water voles, otters, pine martens, and white-tailed eagles, highlighting the importance of wetland renewal. Conservationists believe this project will improve water quality, reduce flood risk, and create more resilient landscapes for future generations.

stories.title: Play area aims to interest children in rare habitat
stories.summary: England's unique chalk streams, home to over 80% of the world's remaining habitats, are facing pressure from prolonged hot and dry weather, threatening the wildlife that inhabits them. A new play area at Winchester Science Centre in Hampshire aims to educate children about the importance of these rare waterways, teaching them about the impact of water flow, temperature, and oxygen on the environment. The centre's exhibit is part of a broader effort to protect these habitats, with the Berkshire, Buckinghamshire & Oxfordshire Wildlife Trust supporting a new group in Westminster to safeguard England's chalk streams.

stories.title: Replacing gas stove can help asthmatics as much as some drugs, US experts find
stories.summary: A US study has found that replacing gas stoves with electric ones can significantly improve asthma symptoms, with a 70% drop in hospital admissions and days off from work or school among participants. The study, which began in December 2024 in Ohio, involved 72 households and found benefits comparable to those reported in clinical trials of commonly used asthma medications. Further research is needed to confirm these findings, but the results suggest a simple and cost-effective solution to alleviate asthma symptoms.
